create.noTaskAccount: No Microsoft or Google account for tasks.

firstRun.title: Welcome to MailClient
firstRun.subtitle: First-time setup — one-time
firstRun.welcomeP1: MailClient syncs email and calendars locally on your computer. When you connect an account, your browser opens — you sign in with Microsoft or Google and see exactly which permissions the app requests.
firstRun.welcomeShield: The technical app registration (Azure / Google Cloud) is handled by the software vendor. You do not need to be an organization admin — unless your IT blocks user consent (then an admin is needed once; see Help).
firstRun.msIntro: The app requests delegated permissions for your signed-in account, including:
firstRun.msLi1: Read and send email and manage folders
firstRun.msLi3: Teams chats (when used by the app)
firstRun.msLi5: Microsoft To Do (read and edit task lists)
firstRun.msDetails: Microsoft shows details in the consent dialog. Sign-in will not work without consent.
firstRun.msConnected: A Microsoft account is already connected.
firstRun.msNoClient: No Microsoft client ID is configured. Under “Custom OAuth app” in Settings you can register an Azure app.
firstRun.googleIntro: The app uses Gmail and Calendar APIs with access to your account, for example:
firstRun.googleLi4: Google Tasks (read and edit task lists)
firstRun.googleDetails: Google shows exact scopes at sign-in. Public distribution often requires Google verification — otherwise only test users in the Cloud Console.
firstRun.googleConnected: A Google account is already connected.
firstRun.googleNoClient: No Google client ID is configured. Under “Custom OAuth app” in Settings you can enter an OAuth client ID (optionally with client secret).
firstRun.doneP1: You can start using the app now or add more accounts later in Settings.
firstRun.doneNoAccount: No account yet — you can start the connection anytime in Settings.
firstRun.errMicrosoft: Microsoft sign-in is not preconfigured for this install. Please enter a client ID in Settings.
firstRun.errGoogle: Google sign-in is not preconfigured for this install. Please enter a client ID in Settings.

workflowIntro.title: Triage folders (In progress / Done)
workflowIntro.body: When you <term>terminate</term> an email (to-do or calendar block), the app can move it to an <wip>“In progress”</wip> folder — no matter which folder it is in right now. When the task is <done>done</done>, the message moves to a <doneFolder>“Done”</doneFolder> folder.
workflowIntro.body2: For each connected mail account, MailClient creates two folders or Gmail labels (or you pick existing ones under <settings>Settings → Mail</settings>). Works for Microsoft 365 and Gmail.
workflowIntro.busyHint: If nothing happens: check whether Microsoft or Google shows sign-in or consent in the <browser>browser</browser> (sometimes behind this window).
workflowIntro.ensureFolders: Create triage folders for all accounts

sidebar.syncFolderOffline: Folder sync requires an internet connection.
sidebar.syncFolderDone: Folder "{{name}}" was updated from the server.

settings.title: Settings
settings.contactsWorkspaceIntro: Use the contacts area to browse, edit, and sync contacts (Microsoft Outlook and Google). More options for this area will be added over time.
settings.contactsGoogleBody: Google sync requires the People API in your Google Cloud project and an OAuth token that includes the contacts scope. After permission changes, remove the Google account under «Accounts» and sign in again.
settings.contactsMicrosoftBody: Outlook contacts sync via Microsoft Graph once a Microsoft account is connected.
settings.contactsAccountsHint: Connect accounts, colors, and re-authentication (for example after new OAuth scopes):
settings.languageHint: Sets the language for menus, settings, and hints. Email content is unchanged.
settings.dashboardGridHint: Each tile’s position, width, and height are multiples of this value (in pixels). Gaps between tiles can still be arbitrary.
settings.dashboardGridRange: Allowed: {{min}}–{{max}} px. Default: {{def}} px. After you change the value (on blur), tiles realign to the new grid.
settings.weatherIntro: Search for a place and save it — the weather tile shows current conditions and the next seven days (Open-Meteo, no API key).
settings.weatherGeocodeEmpty: No match — try a more specific place name.
settings.loginIntro: Usually the app uses the vendor’s preconfigured OAuth app (build environment). You only connect your accounts in the browser. Open the section below only if you want to use your own Azure or Google Cloud registration.
settings.oauthSub: Advanced — store client IDs and optional Google client secret locally
settings.azureIntro: Application (client) ID — only needed if you do not use a preconfigured ID. Saving empty removes the local override (the build default ID applies again, if set).
settings.azureActive: Microsoft client ID is active (preconfigured or saved).
settings.googleOAuthIntro: OAuth client ID from Google Cloud Console (Desktop). Client secret is optional: leave empty for PKCE with a public desktop client (Google native app OAuth). Enable Gmail and Calendar APIs. Loopback redirect:
settings.googlePlaceholderSecretStored: Client secret (leave blank = unchanged or PKCE without secret)
settings.googleActive: Google client ID is active. Client secret is only required if your Google project demands one.
settings.notionIntro: Send mail and events as blocks to Notion pages. If the developer portal won't save: use internal integration (recommended).
settings.notionInternalIntro: notion.so/my-integrations → New integration → Internal. Copy the secret token. On each target page: ⋯ → Connections → Add connection. No redirect URI needed.
settings.notionInternalShareHint: With internal integration: share target pages with the integration in Notion (Connections).
settings.notionOAuthSummary: Public integration (OAuth) — only if redirect URI can be saved
settings.notionOAuthHint: Notion always prepends https:// to redirect URIs. Do not use mailclient:// or http:// — only the “Enter in portal” line. After saving it should show https://127.0.0.1:…
settings.notionConnectDisabledTitle: Save client ID and secret first
settings.notionFavoritesEmpty: No favorites yet. Search for a page below to add one.
settings.backupIntro: Exports the full app configuration (e.g. OAuth client IDs, sync range), the entire renderer local storage (appearance, layout, calendar/mail sidebar, dashboard, etc.), account preferences (color, signature templates, calendar lookahead, order), Notion destinations, and from the local database: mail rules, QuickSteps, templates/snippets, meta folders, workflow boards, VIP senders, triage folders, calendar color overrides, pending scheduled sends, notes including links (calendar/task/note; mail only when the message exists locally). No email bodies, no OAuth tokens — signed-in accounts stay connected; meta folders that pin local folder IDs may need a tweak after a clean reinstall. The file may contain client secrets and should not be shared.
settings.importConfirmBody: The file overwrites app configuration, renderer local storage, mail rules, VIP list, workflow board, and triage folder mappings (if present in the file). Your accounts and sign-ins are preserved; email is not imported. The app will reload afterwards.
settings.localDataIntro: Under %APPDATA%\MailClient you will find the SQLite database (mail, calendar cache, tasks), account tokens (encrypted), images, and the app Chromium cache. For a lightweight settings-only backup use the JSON export above; for a full snapshot of this PC use the ZIP archive.
settings.localDataReclaimable: About {{size}} of cache/temp data can be freed.
settings.localDataAttachmentStale: Including {{size}} stale attachments (older than 7 days — removed on startup and when opening attachments).
settings.localDataOptimizedRestartHint: Chromium cache on disk (~500 MB) is fully removed only after restarting the app — please quit MailClient and open it again.
settings.localDataImportConfirmBody: The ZIP overwrites the entire local app data folder (database, cache, images, config files). The app will restart afterward. Only use archives from the same app or after a fresh install.
settings.msNoClientTitle: No Microsoft client ID (build or settings)
settings.googleConfigureFirstTitle: Configure Google client ID first (General or build default)
settings.accountsIntro: Connect or remove accounts. “Sign in again” opens authentication for the same account (e.g. new API permissions). OAuth details only under General → Custom OAuth app, if needed.
settings.msReconnectTitle: Sign in to Microsoft again (token and permissions)
settings.googleReconnectTitle: Sign in to Google again (refresh token)
settings.clearMailCacheConfirm:
All locally cached mail, folders, and sync state for {{email}} will be deleted. That includes in-app mail To Dos, related notes, and mail↔cloud-task links; for Microsoft accounts, also the cached Outlook master categories. Nothing is changed on the server mailbox.

When online, the app re-syncs immediately afterward. When offline, sync resumes on the next connection (or automatically when the app goes online).
settings.clearMailCacheDoneOnline: Mail cache was cleared and the account was re-synced.
settings.clearMailCacheDoneOffline: Mail cache was cleared. The app will sync again once you are online.
settings.clearTasksCacheConfirm:
All locally cached To Do lists and tasks for {{email}} will be deleted, including sync timestamps and in-app planned times for cloud tasks (calendar/work). Mail↔cloud-task links in the database are kept. Nothing changes on the server (Microsoft To Do / Google Tasks).

When online, the app re-fetches tasks from the cloud in the background (this can take several minutes with very large accounts). When offline, sync resumes on the next connection (or automatically when the app goes online).
settings.clearTasksCacheDoneOnline: Tasks cache was cleared. Lists and tasks are being re-fetched from the cloud in the background (this can take a few minutes for large mailboxes).
settings.clearTasksCacheDoneOffline: Tasks cache was cleared. The app will load tasks again once you are online.
settings.accountOnlineTitle: Open the provider account page in your default browser (password, security, subscription)
settings.syncWindowIntro: Limits how far back new mail is synced. Saves storage and bandwidth for large mailboxes. Already downloaded older mail is kept.
settings.autoImagesHint: Images load directly from servers. For privacy this can enable tracking pixels. When off, you can load images per message manually.
settings.flaggedMailboxFilterHint: For the “Mailbox flag” list filter, only threads count where at least one message is actively flagged outside “Deleted Items” and “Junk”. This trims legacy flags left in deleted/spam-like folders — separate from Microsoft To Do. To remove flags on the server entirely, use Outlook or Graph/PowerShell.
settings.bulkUnflagSettingsHeading: Remove flags on the server (OWA / Outlook)
settings.bulkUnflagSettingsBody: Clears the follow-up / flag state for all flagged messages that are already synced into this app for the selected account, via Microsoft 365 or Gmail APIs. After sync, Outlook on the web and mobile should match. Messages never downloaded here are not touched.
settings.bulkUnflagIntro: Walks flagged messages in the local mail cache for the selected account and updates each one at the provider. OWA and other clients should reflect the change after a short sync.
settings.bulkUnflagNeedAccount: Connect a Microsoft or Google mail account first.
settings.bulkUnflagExcludeDeletedJunk: Only messages outside Deleted Items and Junk (recommended)
settings.bulkUnflagCountUnknown: Re-open the dialog or change options to refresh the count.
settings.bulkUnflagNothing: No flagged messages match the current options.
settings.bulkUnflagConfirm1: Really clear flags for {{count}} messages on the server? This cannot be undone from the app.
settings.bulkUnflagConfirm2: Second confirmation: this bulk action writes to your mailbox.
settings.bulkUnflagOffline: You must be online to run this action.
settings.mailSidebarFoldersIntro: Per account, choose which folders appear in the left mail navigation. Hidden folders remain available (e.g. move, search); the inbox cannot be hidden. Use “Remove from sidebar…” in the folder context menu for a quicker toggle.
settings.triageIntro: When you add a to-do or terminate a message, the app can move it to “In progress” — regardless of its current folder (if mapped). After “Done” (complete to-do) it moves to “Done”. In Gmail, those folders are labels. Without an “In progress” mapping, the message stays where it is.
settings.triageNeedMailAccount: Connect at least one Microsoft or Gmail account under “Accounts”.
settings.wfErrTwoFolders: Please pick two different folders or leave one side empty.
settings.categoriesIntro: Master categories like in Outlook; assign per message in the reader under “Categories”. Managing them requires the “MailboxSettings” permission — reconnect Microsoft after an app update if needed.
settings.categoriesNeedAccount: For Microsoft 365 accounts only. Please connect an account under “Accounts” first.
settings.calendarTzIntro: Controls how event times are shown and the time used when creating Teams meetings against Microsoft 365. “Automatic” uses the Windows system time zone.
settings.calendarApiIntro: Per account: how far from today events are fetched from Microsoft 365 or Google Calendar. A shorter horizon means less data and often faster loads.
settings.calendarConnectHint: Connect a Microsoft or Google account with calendar under “Accounts”.
settings.calendarSidebarIntro: Choose which calendars are listed on the left in Calendar view (same as “Remove from sidebar…” in the context menu). The default calendar slot always stays visible. Calendars are grouped by connected account.
settings.calendarSidebarGroupCalendarsEmpty: No group calendars found or insufficient permissions.

createCloudTask.title: Cloud task from mail
createCloudTask.noAccounts: No Microsoft or Google account connected for tasks.
createCloudTask.otherAccountHint: This message is from an account without tasks. Pick a tasks account.

move.dropWrongAccount: Only messages from the same account as the folder can be moved.

list.emptyTrashConfirm: Permanently delete everything in Deleted Items? Messages will be removed from Microsoft 365 and cannot be recovered.
list.emptyTrashDoneOne: Deleted items emptied (1 message on the server)
list.emptyTrashDoneMany: Deleted items emptied ({{count}} messages on the server)
list.connectOrSelectFolder: Connect an account or select a folder on the left.
list.noConversationsWithTodo: No conversations with an open to-do {{place}}.
list.emptyMeta: No messages match this meta folder.
list.noOpenTodoWithPlace: No conversations with an open to-do {{place}}.

readingPane.emptyNoSelectionBody: Pick a message in the list on the left to read it here.
readingPane.notFoundBody: This message could not be loaded locally.

listViewMenu.filterFlaggedTitle: Counts messages Graph returns with flagStatus “flagged” (not “complete”) — across every folder you sync locally, not just Inbox. Microsoft To Do’s “Flagged email” only shows its current task projection, so a large gap is common. Reconcile with “Clear mail cache”; optionally enable “Ignore Deleted/Junk” under Settings → Mail → Display; bulk-clear flags at the mail source (Outlook / Graph / PowerShell).
listViewMenu.filterMentionedTitle: Mentions will be supported in a later version.

composeTile.needAccount: Connect an account first to send mail from the home page.
composeTile.subject: Subject

tiles.weekSubtitle: Arrows change week · click a day to open the calendar · right-click: new event
tiles.monthSubtitle: Arrows change month · click a day to open the calendar · right-click: new event
tiles.todayTimelineSubtitle: 00:00–24:00 · view aligns to (now − 1 h) · right-click: new event
tiles.todayClockSubtitle: Today · updates every second · full view: calendar today

dashboard.todoEmpty: No messages with a to-do in this view.
dashboard.inboxEmpty: No conversations in the unified inbox.
dashboard.deadlinesEmpty: No due items or flagged threads in the preview.
dashboard.favoritesEmpty: No favourite folders. Star a folder in the sidebar.
dashboard.searchHitsEmpty: Results appear here after you search.
dashboard.calendarNoEvents: No upcoming events in the queried range.

clock.timeZoneSelectAria: Select time zone for second clock

nextMeeting.noCalendars: Connect a Microsoft or Google account with calendar access.
nextMeeting.empty: No upcoming online meeting with a join link in the next 7 days.

weather.configureHint: Set a place under Settings → General for the weather tile.

dashboard.heading: Home
dashboard.todayTimelineNoCalendars: Connect a Microsoft or Google account with calendar access.
dashboard.todayTimelineOutsideWindow: {{count}} more event(s) outside the current view (10 h from now − 1 h)

customTiles.pickKindHint: Choose what this tile should show.
customTiles.pickFolderHint: Pick a folder (preview of recent messages).
customTiles.pickEventHint: Pick an event from the current agenda/week list.
customTiles.pickMailHint: Search for a message (at least 2 characters).
customTiles.noEvents: No events in the list. Sync the calendar or try again later.
customTiles.removeTileTitle: Remove custom tile from the dashboard

dashboardGrid.restoreNothingToast: No saved layout yet. Use the save (floppy) icon first.
dashboardGrid.pinTileTitle: Pin tile at this grid position
dashboardGrid.unpinTileTitle: Unpin tile and return it to the flow layout
dashboardGrid.lastVisibleTileHint: At least one tile must stay visible.
dashboardGrid.marqueeHint: Hold Ctrl (⌘ on Mac) or Shift on empty space and drag a rectangle to select multiple tiles; Shift adds to the current selection (including if you press Shift before releasing). Ctrl+click or Shift+click the grip or tile body toggles a tile in the selection. Press Esc to clear.
dashboardGrid.multiDragHint: Drag any selected tile by its handle to move the whole group together.

header.viewSettingsBody: 30-minute grid, Monday as first day, 07:00–20:00. In day/week: drag in the grid to pick a slot (touch: hold briefly, then drag). More options may come later in Settings.
header.slotDurationShortcuts: Ctrl or ⌘ + Shift+. = finer grid, Ctrl or ⌘ + Shift+, = coarser grid.

shell.emptyPreviewBody: Select a message in the to-do list on the right or an event in the calendar.
shell.eventsIntro: Calendar and mail appointments in the selected range.
shell.sidebarDeleteSectionBody: Delete section «{{name}}»? Assigned calendars will move back to «Unassigned».
shell.sidebarDropUngroupedHint: Drop calendars here to remove them from custom groups.
shell.noLinkedAccount: No account with calendar connected (Microsoft 365 or Google).
shell.groupCalendarsEmptyHint: No group calendars found or missing permissions. In Entra ID, check delegated GroupMember.Read.All and Group.ReadWrite.All (admin consent may be required).
shell.eventSearchHint: ESC closes the dialog. Filtered events stay visible until you clear the search or close the dialog.

errors.missingGraphCalendarId: Calendar ID is missing for this event.
errors.calendarReadOnlyEdit: This calendar does not allow editing.
errors.scheduleParseFailed: Could not determine the time range.
errors.clipboardUnsupported: Copying to the clipboard is not supported here.

eventDialog.allDayNeedDates: All-day: start and end as dates (end exclusive).
eventDialog.endAfterStartExclusive: End must be after start (exclusive end date).
eventDialog.allDayEndLastDayHint: Last calendar day of the event (inclusive).
eventDialog.targetCalendarHint: Microsoft 365 or Google — which calendar receives the new event.
eventDialog.categoriesOutlookOnly: Outlook categories are only available for Microsoft 365 calendars.
eventDialog.categoriesEmptyOutlook: No Outlook categories. Add some under Settings → Mail or in Outlook.
eventDialog.categoriesHint: Selection is sent to Microsoft when you save.
eventDialog.locationSearchEmpty: No matches — try a more specific query.
eventDialog.googleCalendarIdMissing: Calendar ID missing — could not load description.
eventDialog.submitNoAccount: Connect an account with calendar first (Microsoft 365 or Google).
eventDialog.taskDestinationHelpBody: Microsoft To Do or Google Tasks — account and list for the new task.
eventDialog.categoriesMasterHint: The same master categories as for mail (Microsoft Graph).
eventDialog.destinationHelpBody: Storage location in Outlook / Exchange — all calendars of connected accounts in one list.
eventDialog.destinationMoveHelp: Pick another calendar to move this event there when you save.
eventDialog.teamsDisabledAllDay: Teams is not available for all-day events.
eventDialog.attendeesInviteHint: Calendar invitations are sent to all attendees — with or without a Teams meeting.
eventDialog.googleTeamsAttendeesHint: Teams meetings and attendee invitations in MailClient are only supported for Microsoft 365 calendars.
eventDialog.recurrenceHint: Series are available when creating events (Microsoft 365 and Google). Editing the whole series is not implemented yet.
eventDialog.recurrenceUntilInvalid: Please choose a valid end date (YYYY-MM-DD).
eventDialog.recurrenceUntilBeforeStart: The series end date cannot be before the start.
eventDialog.recurrenceCountInvalid: Enter a number between 1 and 999 for the count.
